indicators.title: Fertility rate, total (births per woman)
indicators.shortHook: Where families are still growing fast.

reviewNotes: Iconic demographic-transition map.
Close demographic distractors are acceptable only at hard tiers.

indicators.title: Adolescent fertility rate (births per 1,000 women ages 15-19)
indicators.shortHook: A youth-pattern map with a sharper social signal.

reviewNotes: Strong regional signal and a sharper story than general birth rates.
Avoid pairing with birth-rate or children-share outside Cartographer.

indicators.title: Birth rate, crude (per 1,000 people)
indicators.shortHook: The broadest signal of where populations are adding children.

reviewNotes: Too close to fertility rate and children-share for Daily.
Still useful as a Practice comparison map for demographic transition.

indicators.title: Death rate, crude (per 1,000 people)

reviewNotes: Crude death rate is easy to misread because age structure dominates.
Good lesson map, not a default Daily map yet.

indicators.title: Life expectancy at birth, total (years)
indicators.shortHook: A familiar health map with strong regional anchors.

reviewNotes: Classic health map with fair global anchors.
Avoid pairing with adult mortality as an easy-tier distractor.

indicators.title: Mortality rate, infant (per 1,000 live births)
indicators.shortHook: A child-health map with unmistakable low and high anchors.

reviewNotes: Almost identical to under-5 mortality.
Practice only so players can learn the distinction.

indicators.title: Mortality rate, under-5 (per 1,000 live births)
indicators.shortHook: A stark map of child survival.

reviewNotes: Strong child-survival map and clearer than infant mortality for Daily.
Infant mortality is acceptable only as a hard-tier close distractor.

indicators.title: Mortality rate, adult, male (per 1,000 male adults)
indicators.shortHook: A mortality map with conflict, health, and risk-behavior clues.

reviewNotes: Distinctive enough for expert play because male risk patterns matter.
Too correlated with other mortality maps for default Daily.

indicators.title: Mortality rate, adult, female (per 1,000 female adults)
indicators.shortHook: A parallel mortality map where gender differences matter.

reviewNotes: Very close to life expectancy and male adult mortality.
Playable only as an expert mortality puzzle.

indicators.title: Population ages 0-14 (% of total population)

reviewNotes: Too correlated with birth and fertility maps for default Daily.
Useful Practice anchor for age-structure learning.

indicators.title: Population ages 65 and above (% of total population)

reviewNotes: Readable aging map and strong contrast to youth-heavy regions.
Do not pair with birth-rate as a low-tier distractor.

indicators.title: Age dependency ratio (% of working-age population)
indicators.shortHook: A map of who supports whom.

reviewNotes: Good synthesis of youth and aging pressure.
Fair when distractors avoid pure fertility lookalikes.

indicators.title: Rural population (% of total population)
indicators.shortHook: The inverse of the urban world.

reviewNotes: Exact inverse of urban population creates filler rather than a new puzzle.
Retired in favor of urban population.

indicators.title: Urban population (% of total population)

reviewNotes: Core settlement geography map.
Retire rural inverse to keep this version from feeling duplicated.

indicators.title: Population density (people per sq. km of land area)
indicators.shortHook: A map where small crowded places shout.

reviewNotes: Highly map-native and strategically probeable.
Small crowded countries make this satisfying.

indicators.title: School enrollment, primary (% gross)
indicators.shortHook: A schooling map with a surprisingly noisy edge.

reviewNotes: Gross enrollment can exceed intuitive school attendance readings.
Coverage is thinner and map pattern needs editorial review.

indicators.title: School enrollment, secondary (% gross)

reviewNotes: Stronger education signal than primary enrollment.
Coverage is just adequate, so keep missing-data caveat visible.

indicators.title: School enrollment, tertiary (% gross)
indicators.shortHook: A higher-education map with rich-country and city-state clues.

reviewNotes: Interesting higher-education map but uses an older selected year.
Expert-only until year freshness improves.

indicators.title: Government expenditure on education, total (% of GDP)
indicators.shortHook: A budget-priority map, not a wealth map.

reviewNotes: Budget-share map can surprise players but does not equal quality.
Needs careful caveat in reveal.

indicators.title: Current health expenditure (% of GDP)

reviewNotes: Good non-obvious health-system spending puzzle.
Reveal caveat prevents reading it as health quality.

indicators.title: Out-of-pocket expenditure (% of current health expenditure)
indicators.shortHook: A map of who pays at the clinic.

reviewNotes: Interesting health-finance map but not intuitive enough for default Daily.
Keep for expert players who enjoy system-design clues.

indicators.title: People using at least basic drinking water services (% of population)
indicators.shortHook: A basic-infrastructure map with clear missing edges.

reviewNotes: Clear basic-infrastructure story.
Avoid same-day pairing with electricity and sanitation when possible.

indicators.title: People using at least basic sanitation services (% of population)

reviewNotes: Readable infrastructure map with clear development signal.
Do not pair closely with clean fuels, water access, or electricity on easier tiers.

indicators.title: Incidence of tuberculosis (per 100,000 people)
indicators.shortHook: A disease-burden map with distinctive regional pockets.

reviewNotes: Distinct disease-burden map that is not just GDP.
Good expert-style Daily with clear source/year.

indicators.title: Individuals using the Internet (% of population)

reviewNotes: Readable connectivity map with strong regional anchors.
High development correlation means income-style distractors need restraint.

indicators.title: Mobile cellular subscriptions (per 100 people)
indicators.shortHook: A connectivity map where values can exceed 100.

reviewNotes: Interesting because values can exceed 100 subscriptions per 100 people.
Distinct from internet users and fixed broadband.

indicators.title: Fixed broadband subscriptions (per 100 people)

reviewNotes: Useful contrast with mobile-first connectivity.
Coverage and infrastructure nuance make it expert-only.

indicators.title: Secure Internet servers (per 1 million people)

reviewNotes: Good outlier map for hosting and finance hubs.
Too technical for default Daily.

indicators.title: Access to electricity (% of population)

reviewNotes: Clear infrastructure map with strong geographic anchors.
Avoid same-day pairing with water access and sanitation.

indicators.title: Access to clean fuels and technologies for cooking (% of population)

reviewNotes: A household-energy map with a real public-health story.
High income correlation requires careful distractors.

indicators.title: Renewable energy consumption (% of total final energy consumption)
indicators.shortHook: A map where low-income biomass and modern renewables can both matter.

reviewNotes: Traditional biomass caveat makes the map counterintuitive.
Expert-only until the reveal copy can carry the nuance.

indicators.title: Renewable electricity output (% of total electricity output)

reviewNotes: Good hydro/grid-mix geography puzzle.
Keep distinct from total renewable energy consumption.

indicators.title: Carbon dioxide (CO2) emissions excluding LULUCF per capita (t CO2e/capita)

reviewNotes: High-interest climate map with resource-economy outliers.
GHG per capita is acceptable only as a hard-tier close distractor.

indicators.title: Total greenhouse gas emissions excluding LULUCF per capita (t CO2e/capita)

reviewNotes: Interesting broader climate footprint map.
Too close to CO2 per capita for Daily without a very deliberate hard-tier setup.

indicators.title: PM2.5 air pollution, mean annual exposure (micrograms per cubic meter)

reviewNotes: Strong air-pollution geography with recognizable regional belts.
One of the cleanest Daily environmental maps.

indicators.title: Forest area (% of land area)

reviewNotes: Excellent map-led geography puzzle.
Strong contrast among humid tropics, boreal regions, islands, and arid states.

indicators.title: Arable land (% of land area)

reviewNotes: Good physical-geography and farming-belt reasoning.
Works best with probes in deserts, dense deltas, and temperate crop regions.

indicators.title: Agricultural land (% of land area)

reviewNotes: Useful land-use practice map.
Less Daily-worthy than arable land or forest area because pasture broadens the definition.

indicators.title: Cereal yield (kg per hectare)

reviewNotes: Distinctive agricultural productivity pattern.
Excellent expert-style Daily when reveal explains crop mix caveat.

indicators.title: Agriculture, forestry, and fishing, value added (% of GDP)

reviewNotes: Strong economy-structure map that is not a raw-total GDP quiz.
Avoid unfair pairing with agricultural employment.

indicators.title: GDP per capita (current US$)

reviewNotes: Familiar baseline map and useful anchor for other reveals.
Do not pair with PPP outside hard tiers.

indicators.title: GDP per capita, PPP (current international $)
indicators.shortHook: Income per person adjusted for local prices.

reviewNotes: Too close to nominal GDP per capita for default Daily.
Good Practice comparison for purchasing-power nuance.

indicators.title: Inflation, consumer prices (annual %)

reviewNotes: Volatile macro stress map can be fun but one-year shocks dominate.
Expert-only until editorial notes are more country-specific.

indicators.title: Unemployment, total (% of total labor force) (modeled ILO estimate)
indicators.shortHook: A labor-market map that is not just poverty.

reviewNotes: Readable labor-market map with non-obvious regional pockets.
Definition caveat helps distinguish it from poverty or non-work.

indicators.title: Labor force participation rate, total (% of total population ages 15+) (modeled ILO estimate)
indicators.shortHook: A map of who is counted in the labor force.

reviewNotes: Useful labor concept but less visually memorable than unemployment or agricultural employment.
Practice-only for now.

indicators.title: Labor force participation rate, female (% of female population ages 15+) (modeled ILO estimate)

reviewNotes: Valuable gendered labor map.
Needs expert context because social norms and measurement vary.

indicators.title: Employment in agriculture (% of total employment) (modeled ILO estimate)

reviewNotes: Strong work-structure map that rewards reasoning about sector mix.
Do not pair with employment-services on easier tiers.

indicators.title: Employment in services (% of total employment) (modeled ILO estimate)

reviewNotes: Nearly inverse of agricultural employment.
Useful in Practice, not Daily.

indicators.title: Vulnerable employment, total (% of total employment) (modeled ILO estimate)

reviewNotes: Near-identical to self-employed workers and hard to explain quickly.
Retired until the labor catalog is hand-curated.

indicators.title: Self-employed, total (% of total employment) (modeled ILO estimate)

reviewNotes: Near-identical to vulnerable employment.
Needs editorial decision before returning to playable catalog.

indicators.title: Trade (% of GDP)

reviewNotes: Good open-economy map with small-state and port-economy clues.
Exports/imports only acceptable as hard-tier close distractors.

indicators.title: Exports of goods and services (% of GDP)
indicators.shortHook: A map of economies selling outward.

reviewNotes: Close to trade share and imports share.
Playable only as a hard open-economy distinction.

indicators.title: Imports of goods and services (% of GDP)
indicators.shortHook: A map of economies buying from abroad.

reviewNotes: Close to trade and exports share.
Keep as Expert Practice until open-economy distractors are hand-tuned.

indicators.title: Foreign direct investment, net inflows (% of GDP)

reviewNotes: Volatile but interesting for finance/resource-flow players.
Too one-year-spiky for default Daily.

indicators.title: Current account balance (% of GDP)

reviewNotes: Interesting for macro nerds but too abstract for default Daily.
Keep in Expert Practice only.

indicators.title: Tax revenue (% of GDP)

reviewNotes: Coverage is exactly at the floor and reference year is older than most catalog items.
Hold until fiscal-capacity copy and data caveat are reviewed.
